title: Dryopteris maxima
intro: Dryopteris maxima, also known as the giant shield fern, is a species of fern native to East Asia, characterized by its large, leathery fronds and distinctive shield-shaped leaflets. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Dryopteris maxima include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infused in tea, and is also used to make capsules and tablets for the treatment of various health conditions.
taxonomy: Dryopteris maxima is classified as a species of fern within the genus Dryopteris, a group of temperate and subarctic ferns. The genus Dryopteris is a part of the family Dryopteridaceae, a group of ferns commonly known as the wood ferns. Dryopteris maxima specifically belongs to the subfamily Dryopteridoideae. The kingdom Plantae encompasses the entire plant kingdom, including ferns like Dryopteris maxima. This species is also known to be native to the mountainous regions of East Asia, including China, Japan, and Korea.
